disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive exertional dyspnoea and severe fatigue over 3-4 months, acutely worse over two weeks, limiting basic activities
trigger_question_examples: How far can you walk before getting breathless, and how has it changed?
How long has this been building up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Months of getting more breathless, but the last two weeks I can barely walk to the kitchen without getting winded, and I'm exhausted.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New orthopnoea (needs three pillows) with occasional nocturnal cough
trigger_question_examples: Can you lie flat, or do you need to prop up to breathe?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can't lie flat now - I need three pillows, and I get a bit of a cough at night.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild bilateral ankle oedema by the end of the day
trigger_question_examples: Any swelling in the ankles or legs?
Do your ankles swell by evening?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My ankles get a bit puffy by the end of the day.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain and no palpitations
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or a racing/irregular heartbeat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain, and I haven't noticed my heart racing or skipping.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this just getting older and unfit, or something with my heart?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that breathlessness at rest/lying flat with ankle swelling over a short period is not simple deconditioning - it suggests fluid backing up into the lungs, likely from a heart-valve/heart-function problem, and needs urgent same-day assessment with a heart scan and blood tests, not reassurance.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can you just send me a water tablet and I'll see my GP next week?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that a remote water tablet is not safe management - the breathlessness at rest needs urgent in-person assessment with blood tests (kidney function/salts), a heart scan (echocardiogram) and a plan, and cannot wait a week.

deflection_behaviours.description: Patient may attribute decline to ageing/deconditioning - tests whether the AI Doctor recognises valvular decompensation and escalates.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I'm probably just getting old and out of shape.
